item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fidget spinner Beyblade stands. I love SCP.
This stands out on its own but could easily fit into any GOI and has potential for Tales. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm concerned that the term "transmogrify" does not necessarily fit with the clinical tone of an SCP, as much as I otherwise enjoy the article. "Transform", maybe? "Convert"?

item.title: Re: It's time to D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-Dauthor commentary!
item.content:encoded: I'm absolutely in LOVE with this article, and it got my upvote before I even finished it. However, I'm seconding Geckoguy's sentiment of wanting a footnote defining what a Smilodon is- I had to google it myself, which took some of the immersion out of the article. Even still, fantastic work.

item.title: Re: It's time to D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-Dauthor commentary!
item.content:encoded: One REALLY tiny nitpick is that you didn't define the first latin bit, but thats so minor i dont even know why i care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One sentence into the description and I can already tell that this is gonna be good.

item.title: It's time to D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-D-Dauthor commentary!
item.content:encoded:
This is born of my childhood fascination with Shonen battle game anime— specifically, the trope you see in a lot of them where not only are the magical beasts in the cards/tops/marbles real, but they're capable of having measurable effects on the environment despite being holographic/otherwise immaterial. Seriously, Duel Monsters is a dangerous game even if you're not battling on the back of a blimp with an Egyptian pharaoh.

Anyway, it's half that, half the fact that fidget spinner SCPs haven't really… worked in the past. I feel like that a lot of the missed the point that they're meant to be toys and little else; so, to skippify them, you want to capitalize on the fact that they're not only toys, but fad toys. Don't tell me that Beyblade wasn't a fad.

Special thanks to DrChandra, stormbreath, Jazstar, A_Sack_Of_Potatoes, and everyone else on chat who critiqued it.
